lesson.menu_desc: Acknowledging concerns and concession connectors: however, nevertheless -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Handling Objections -- Acknowledging Concerns & Concession Connectors
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Simone Quiles de Santana Marques | Aula 19 -- Handling Objections

listenings.text: Hi Simone, this is Daniel again, with a quick note before your call with the client this afternoon. They have three objections. First, they think the price is too high. Second, they are reluctant to sign a long contract. Third, they have a hesitation about the delivery date. Here is how to handle each one. Do not argue. First, acknowledge the concern: say that it is a valid point. Then add your answer with a connector. The price is higher than the market, however our quality is much better. They are reluctant about a long contract, nevertheless the longer term protects them from price changes. They worry about delivery, but on the other hand our record is excellent and we can reassure them with a guarantee. Stay calm, listen first, and show that you are willing to find a solution. If you acknowledge each concern before you answer, you will overcome the objection. Good luck.

extra_audio.text: First, the client raises an objection and Simone listens carefully without interrupting. Next, Simone acknowledges the concern and says it is a valid point, so the client feels heard. Then Simone answers with a concession connector, such as however or nevertheless, to add her side of the story. After that, she reassures the client and shows she is willing to find a solution. Finally, the client is no longer reluctant, the hesitation is gone, and together they overcome the objection.
